CURRENCIES_EXCHANGE_XRP.TEXT_3: It is the 3rd largest cryptocurrency by market cap. It is also one of the oldest cryptocurrencies in existence, because the idea is a little older than Bitcoin itself. In 2005, Jed McCaleb came up with the idea of a financial services system that would act as a secure mode of payment for online transactions. He then approached Arthur Britto and David Schwartz, who built it with the help of Ryan Fugger, who as far back as 2005, had built such a system and named it Opencoin. It is the Opencoin system that formed the basis of Ripplelabs, and the cryptocurrency that debuted in the crypto markets as Ripple in 2012. Like most cryptocurrencies, XRP became most visible to the masses during the 2017 crypto rally. At the time, the crypto rallied to a high of over $3.5 on December 29th, 2017, and with a market cap of $73 billion. Like other cryptocurrencies, it has since corrected and currently has a market cap of about $13.67 billion.
CURRENCIES_EXCHANGE_XRP.TEXT_5: The currencies ledger has several features that put it at the forefront of the crypto revolution. One of its key features is speed. This currency is one of the fastest ways to send money globally. One can send money using it to any part of the world in under 4 seconds. Related to speed is the feature of scalability. A major advantage that centralized systems such as Visa have over cryptos is that of scalability. Most blockchains can handle very few transactions per second, making them impractical for large scale use in the finance industry. XPR does not have this problem and can consistently handle more than 1500 transactions per second. In fact, as of July 15th, 2019, it could handle over 65,000 transactions per second, making it competitive as a global payment system. The other key feature is decentralization. In the past, there were concerns that XRP is a centralized coin where RippleLabs controls all the validator nodes. This notion has since been dispelled as the network is run by more than 150 independent validators spread across the world. It is also for this reason that back in 2018, there was a rebranding from Ripple (XRP) to XRP (XRP). The goal was to help investors understand the difference between the company and the coin. Lastly, it is eco-friendly. Unlike proof-of-work coins that require energy to produce, the currency’s ledger settles transactions without mining.

DEFI_PORTAL.PROJECT_INFO_MKR_ARTICLE: Maker is a DeFi platform that is used to maintain the DAI stablecoin. DAI coin is based on smart contracts powered by Ethereum and Maker is a decentralized finance tool that stabilizes the price of DAI by creating new MKRs. DeFi protocols in place regulate the price of the stablecoin in a transparent way with collateralized loans. MKR as a decentralized finance instrument is one of the oldest on the market - it was created in 2014 and has since earned its popularity in the crypto community at large.
DEFI_PORTAL.PROJECT_INFO_LEND_ARTICLE: Aave is a DeFi protocol that is used to offer instant credit. Based on Ethereum, LEND is a decentralized finance tool working on a collateralized lending system. DeFi makes borrowing money simple - lenders provide liquidity by depositing money into a pool, while borrowers that need a loan have to provide collateral in other currency. Aave has been a household name in decentralized finance space since 2017, and has grown significantly over the years it’s been on the market.
DEFI_PORTAL.PROJECT_INFO_SNX_ARTICLE: Synthetics Network Token is a decentralized finance protocol based on Ethereum. SNX’s DeFi system is similar to Maker, where the platform’s tokens are used to create assets called Synths. Those synthetic assets copy the price of ‘real world’ assets. To buy and trade those, one has to use SNX - the decentralized finance token of the platform. The Synthetix DeFi project initially started as a conventional stablecoin Havven that got rebranded in late 2018.
DEFI_PORTAL.PROJECT_INFO_COMP_ARTICLE: Compound is a decentralized finance lending platform that supports multiple cryptocurrencies. Its native DeFi asset COMP (or cToken) is used as a basis of the Compound DeFi platform. Ethereum-based Compound offers decentralized governance on a blockchain. To vote for any changes to be made on the decentralized finance platform, COMP tokens should be bought with fiat or other cryptocurrencies.
DEFI_PORTAL.PROJECT_INFO_KNC_ARTICLE: An Ethereum-based Kyber Network is a decentralized finance project that provides instantaneous token exchange. Kyber Network’s DeFi protocol supports 70+ ERC20 projects and swiftness of conversion is provided by crypto funds called reserves. In order to use reserve pools, one has to pay a fee in the ecosystem’s native token, KNC. Decentralized finance projects like Kyber make usage of different dapps easy, as you don’t have to wait for token swaps and tools like KNC bring DeFi closer to everyday adoption of crypto payments.

BITCOIN_HALVING.FAQ.ITEMS.5.QUESTION: What Happens to Miners When the Bitcoin Reward is Halved?
BITCOIN_HALVING.FAQ.ITEMS.6.QUESTION: What Happens When All 21 Million Bitcoins Have Been Mined?
BITCOIN_HALVING.FAQ.ITEMS.5.ANSWER: Miners face a significant challenge as the reward for their efforts is reduced, potentially impacting the profitability of mining operations. This adjustment period encourages technological innovation and efficiency improvements among miners, who must optimize their operations to maintain profitability under the new reward structure. Some miners may exit the market, leading to a temporary reduction in the network's hash rate until equilibrium is reached.
BITCOIN_HALVING.FAQ.ITEMS.6.ANSWER: Reaching the 21 million cap is a long-term prospect, expected around the year 2140. After this point, miners will no longer receive new bitcoins for block rewards and will rely solely on transaction fees as compensation for their role in processing transactions and securing the Bitcoin network. This shift could fundamentally change the economics of mining and Bitcoin's security model.
BITCOIN_HALVING.FAQ.ITEMS.7.QUESTION: Why Does Bitcoin Halve?
BITCOIN_HALVING.FAQ.ITEMS.7.ANSWER: The halving mechanism is Bitcoin's solution to prevent inflation and ensure a controlled supply, similar to the way central banks manage the supply of traditional currencies. By halving the block reward, Bitcoin ensures that its total supply will never exceed 21 million coins, making it a deflationary asset by design. This scarcity is a key factor driving Bitcoin's adoption and valuation as a digital store of value.

PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.DESCRIPTION: More possibilities with just one address. Instantly streamline your swaps. Registration free.
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.WHAT_YOU_GET.DES: Try our flexible solution both for personal use and for business.
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.WHAT_YOU_GET.ITE: Always exchange to one address and don't waste your time on creating new swaps.
Operate one address for all your swap needs.
Effortlessly send and receive your desired cryptocurrency.
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.HOW_IT_WORKS.STE: Select an exchange pair and enter your payout address – we will remember them for all future transactions
Send your funds to the generated address – it will work as a permanent deposit address created just for you
Receive the chosen asset to your address as many times as you wish and track your exchange history
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.PERFECT_FOR_USER: Here are some of the use cases for Permanent Exchange Addresses

Once you set your preferred exchange pair, you don't have to manually create new exchanges ever again – use the generated address for all future transactions with this pair, including cross-chain swaps.
Save the deposit address in your Web3 wallet and use it as a handy tool to create instant swaps with your selected asset pair.
Use your payout address to instantly top-up your balance on centralized or decentralized exchanges in your currency of choice.
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.TITLE: FAQ
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.0.ANSW: Yes! All ChangeNOW assets are interexchangeable, including cross-chain swaps. You can create a Permanent Exchange with any asset pair supported on ChangeNOW.

PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.PERFECT_FOR_USER: Receive uninterrupted crypto payments with automatic conversion into your target currency.
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.6.QUES: What will happen if I occasionally send funds in a wrong asset?
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.6.ANSW: No worries, we’ve got you! Depending on your particular case, we can either process the transaction normally or suggest a refund to your address. To resolve cases like this quicker, feel free to reach out to us at support@changenow.io, we’re here for you 24/7.
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.EXCHANGES_SIMPLE: Use our crypto exchange calculator to see the current estimated rate.
The exchange starts the second your deposit is confirmed. The rate is set according to the current market.
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.7.QUES: Can I use Permanent Addresses with ChangeNOW API?
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.7.ANSW:
Yes – as a partner, you can operate Permanent Exchange Addresses through our API and use them as a business. You can see API documentation here. There are several ways you can benefit from our solutions. For example, you can automatically convert the payments you receive into your target currency without any manual operations. More use cases are outlined on this page above.

For more info, you are always welcome to reach out to us at partners@changenow.io, we will be happy to answer any questions you’re interested in.

PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.1.QUES: What exchange fees apply to Permanent Addresses?
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.1.ANSW: The fees are the same you would see with a regular exchange: network fees and service fees. They are already included in the estimated rate you see. We guarantee no extra charges in the process – what you see is what you get.
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.2.QUES: What is the maximum amount I can exchange?
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.2.ANSW: There is no limit to the amount you send – the system will process the exact amount of funds that was sent to the deposit address and pay it out to you in the target currency.
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.3.ANSW: Sure! Our minimal amounts start with just $2 depending on the exchange pair.
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.4.QUES: Can I share my Permanent Addresses with other individuals?
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.4.ANSW: Yes – the sender and the receiver of the funds do not have to be the same person. Use this to convert automated deposits instantly and receive the funds directly in your target currency.
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.5.QUES: How can I track the process and view my exchange history?
PERMANENT_EXCHANGE_PAGE.FAQ.ITEMS.5.ANSW:
The process can be tracked on the exchange page once you create it. Your exchange history is shown on the same page, so we recommend to bookmark it.
Additionally, all of your exchange history is stored in your Pro account – sign up and track you transactions with ease.

BITCOIN_HALVING.TIMER.ADDITIONAL_TEXT: Gear up for the monumental Bitcoin Halving 2024, a cornerstone event in the Bitcoin ecosystem, when the reward for mining bitcoins will be halved from 6.25 to 3.125 bitcoins. This event is pivotal, influencing Bitcoin’s inflation rate, scarcity, and potentially its market value. Let's dive  into the heart of Bitcoin's evolution with the 2024 Bitcoin Halving, exploring its impact on Bitcoin investment, Bitcoin's blockchain technology, and the broader financial markets.
BITCOIN_HALVING.HOW_IT_WORKS.INTRO_TEXT: The Bitcoin Halving 2024 event stands on the horizon as a monumental occurrence within the cryptocurrency realm, symbolizing a crucial phase in the Bitcoin network's lifecycle that enthusiasts, investors, and financial strategists eagerly anticipate. This event, integral to Bitcoin's unique economic model, serves to maintain its deflationary nature by methodically decreasing the rate at which new bitcoins are introduced to the market. Specifically, the Bitcoin Halving 2024 will witness the reward for mining new blocks being halved from the current 6.25 bitcoins to 3.125 bitcoins, a change that directly affects miners but also has broader implications for the Bitcoin market and its valuation.
BITCOIN_HALVING.HOW_IT_WORKS.TEXTS.0.DES: A Bitcoin halving event is a fundamental aspect of Bitcoin's design, occurring every four years, after all 210,000 blocks are mined. This mechanism ensures a gradual reduction in the rate of new bitcoins generated, extending the total time it will take for all bitcoins to be mined. It's a critical feature that helps combat inflation and mimic the natural scarcity properties of physical commodities such as  gold, making Bitcoin a 'digital gold' for the modern era. The halving is not just a technical event; it's an important moment that has historically influenced Bitcoin's price and various aspects of the cryptocurrency ecosystem.
BITCOIN_HALVING.HOW_IT_WORKS.TEXTS.1.TIT: When is the Next Bitcoin Halving?
BITCOIN_HALVING.HOW_IT_WORKS.TEXTS.1.DES: Projected to occur in the third week of April 2024, the exact date of the next Bitcoin halving depends on the mining activity and can slightly vary. Cryptocurrency platforms and analysts closely monitor the Bitcoin blockchain to provide accurate countdowns and forecasts, preparing investors for the event. This anticipation builds a unique market environment where speculation, strategy, and historical data converge to forecast the potential outcomes of the halving.
BITCOIN_HALVING.DISCLAIMER: Disclaimer: Nothing presented on this page represents any kind of investment advice and is merely the opinion of the author. Investing into crypto is a high-risk activity and should be done only after extensive research.

FAQ.Q26_TEXT_1: The Bitcoin Cash hard fork has resulted in creating multiple chains. However, as they are not secured by replay protection technologies, there is a chance of double-spending affecting both chains (BCH/BSV or BCH/BCHA). It means that transactions made with one of these coins may be replicated for the other.

Your card must comply with the requirements of our purchase provider, Simplex: the card must be issued by VISA or MasterCard. It may be a pre-paid (make sure to check if your pre-paid card is eligible for crypto-related purchases).
